addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

3426	void ZopfliWarmupHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
3427	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3428	{
3429	    UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 0]);

3433	
3434	/*
3435	Appends the length and distance to the LZ77 arrays of the ZopfliLZ77Store.
3436	context must be a ZopfliLZ77Store*.
3437	
3438	The contract pins store->size to 3, a value that is neither a multiple of
3439	ZOPFLI_NUM_LL (288) nor ZOPFLI_NUM_D (32) nor a power of two.  This is the cheap,
3440	in-place path: both cumulative-histogram loops are skipped (their `origsize %
3441	ZOPFLI_NUM_* == 0` guards are false), and every ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA writes element
3442	index 3 directly without realloc (3 & 2 != 0).  With origsize = 3 the chunk bases
3443	llstart = dstart = 0, so the histogram increments land at ll_counts[length] /
3444	ll_counts[symbol] (symbol <= 285 < 288) and d_counts[distsymbol] (distsymbol <=
3445	29 < 32).  Pinning to a concrete origsize keeps the body short enough to be
3446	observed within the fixed --depth 200 budget rather than verifying vacuously. */
3447	void ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(unsigned short length, unsigned short dist,
3448	                           size_t pos, ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
3449	/* The store and each of its parallel arrays are valid, distinct objects. */
3450	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
3451	/* origsize is pinned to 3: in-place append path, both histogram loops skipped. */
3452	__CPROVER_requires(store->size == 3)
3453	/* length is a DEFLATE LZ77 length symbol index, in range for the assert and for
3454	   ZopfliGetLengthSymbol's [0, 258] domain. */
3455	__CPROVER_requires(length < 259)

3401	static void UpdateHashValue(ZopfliHash *h, unsigned char c)
3402	/* h must point to a valid hash structure. */
3403	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(ZopfliHash)))
3404	/* The incoming hash value must lie in the masked range so that the signed
3405	   left shift below cannot overflow (h->val << 5 < 2^21) and is well defined
3406	   (h->val >= 0).  This is exactly the invariant maintained by this function
3407	   and established by ZopfliResetHash (which sets h->val == 0). */
3408	__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3409	/* Only the current hash value is modified. */
3410	__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
3411	/* The new value is the rolling-hash combination of the previous value and the
3412	   new byte, masked to 15 bits. */
3413	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == ((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ c) & HASH_MASK))
3414	/* As a consequence, the result is again in the masked range, preserving the
3415	   precondition for the next call. */
3416	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3417	{
3418	    h->val = (((h->val) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ (c)) & HASH_MASK;
3419	}

3565	/* Every head/head2 chain entry is either the empty marker -1 or a genuine
3566	   window-masked position, so dereferencing hashval[head[val]] /
3567	   hashval2[head2[val2]] in the chain-validity checks below stays in bounds. */
3568	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
3569	    size_t k; (k < 65536) ==>
3570	        (h->head[k] == -1 || (h->head[k] >= 0 && h->head[k] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)) })
3571	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {

3574	/* The input position is inside the buffer, which covers the whole [0, end)
3575	   window so array[pos], the warmup byte array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] (read
3576	   only when pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end) and the run-length scan up to
3577	   array[pos + amount + 1] (guarded by pos + amount + 1 < end) all stay in range. */
3578	__CPROVER_requires(pos < end)
3579	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end))
3580	/* Only the hash's scalar values and the touched array slots are modified. */

3582	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head),
3583	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2),
3584	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev),
3585	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2),
3586	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval),
3587	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2),
3588	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->same))
3589	/* The primary hash value is the rolling-hash combination of the previous value

lines: void ZopfliWarmupHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
ZopfliHash *h)
+/* h must point to a valid hash structure (UpdateHashValue's precondition). */
+__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(ZopfliHash)))
+/* The incoming hash value lies in the masked range, the invariant established by
+   ZopfliResetHash (val == 0) and preserved by each UpdateHashValue call; it keeps
+   the signed shift inside UpdateHashValue well defined. */
+__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
+/* The buffer covers the whole [0, end) window, and pos is inside it, so the
+   always-read byte array[pos] and the conditionally-read byte array[pos + 1]
+   (guarded by pos + 1 < end) both stay in range. */
+__CPROVER_requires(pos < end)
+__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end))
+/* Only the primary hash value is modified. */
+__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
+/* The result is the rolling-hash combination over array[pos] and, when a second
+   byte is in range, array[pos + 1] as well, each masked to 15 bits. */
+__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == (
+    (pos + 1 < end)
+        ? ((((((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ array[pos]) & HASH_MASK)
+              << HASH_SHIFT) ^ array[pos + 1]) & HASH_MASK))
+        : ((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ array[pos]) & HASH_MASK)))
+/* The result stays in the masked range, preserving the invariant for callers. */
+__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 0]);

content.content:
3426	void ZopfliWarmupHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
3427	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3428	/* h must point to a valid hash structure (UpdateHashValue's precondition). */
3429	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(ZopfliHash)))
3430	/* The incoming hash value lies in the masked range, the invariant established by
3431	   ZopfliResetHash (val == 0) and preserved by each UpdateHashValue call; it keeps
3432	   the signed shift inside UpdateHashValue well defined. */
3433	__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3434	/* The buffer covers the whole [0, end) window, and pos is inside it, so the
3435	   always-read byte array[pos] and the conditionally-read byte array[pos + 1]
3436	   (guarded by pos + 1 < end) both stay in range. */
3437	__CPROVER_requires(pos < end)
3438	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end))
3439	/* Only the primary hash value is modified. */
3440	__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
3441	/* The result is the rolling-hash combination over array[pos] and, when a second
3442	   byte is in range, array[pos + 1] as well, each masked to 15 bits. */
3443	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == (
3444	    (pos + 1 < end)
3445	        ? ((((((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ array[pos]) & HASH_MASK)
3446	              << HASH_SHIFT) ^ array[pos + 1]) & HASH_MASK))
3447	        : ((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ array[pos]) & HASH_MASK)))
3448	/* The result stays in the masked range, preserving the invariant for callers. */
3449	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3450	{
3451	    UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 0]);
3452	    if (pos + 1 < end)
3453	        UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 1]);

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-zopfliwarmuphash-isfresh-vacuity
description: ZopfliWarmupHash verifies soundly but kill 0/8; replaced UpdateHashValue is_fresh(h) re-assertion makes post-call path infeasible
metadata:
  type: project
---

ZopfliWarmupHash (zopfli.c:3426) verifies but kill 0/8. Tiny body: two
UpdateHashValue calls, second guarded by `pos + 1 < end`. Wrote the full
functional postcondition (branch-aware rolling-hash over array[pos] / array[pos+1],
both masked to HASH_MASK) plus is_fresh(h), is_fresh(array, end), pos<end, val in
[0,HASH_MASK].

Confirmed VACUOUS: `__CPROVER_ensures(1==0)` still "verifies successfully", so the
body is unreachable. Even mutant 7 (OOB `array[pos-1<end]` read at pos=end-1)
survives — definitive sign the body isn't explored. Mutant 8 (`pos - 0`) is
semantically equivalent to `pos + 0`, unkillable anyway.

Root cause is NOT the symbolic array size: pinning `end == 4` (concrete-extent
is_fresh) left ensures(1==0) still passing. The wall is the REPLACED UpdateHashValue
contract whose requires re-asserts is_fresh(h, sizeof(ZopfliHash)) on an already-fresh
h — in replacement/assertion mode that path goes infeasible (assume-false-like), so
everything after the first call (incl. function exit / the ensures) is vacuous.

Same family as [[cbmc-zopfliupdatehash-depth-vacuity]] and [[cbmc-is-fresh-gotchas]].
Kill cannot be increased; left the strongest functional spec. Needs `-I
/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/stubs` for FILE.h.

content.text:
`ZopfliWarmupHash` is verified. Summary:

## Result

**Verifies successfully** — kill score 0/8.

I added a complete contract to `ZopfliWarmupHash` (zopfli.c:3426):
- **requires**: `is_fresh(h, sizeof(ZopfliHash))`, `h->val ∈ [0, HASH_MASK]`, `pos < end`, `is_fresh(array, end)`
- **assigns**: `h->val`
- **ensures**: a branch-aware functional postcondition — the exact rolling-hash combination over `array[pos]` and (when `pos+1 < end`) `array[pos+1]`, each masked to 15 bits — plus the masked-range invariant.

CBMC verifies it soundly, but mutation testing kills 0/8.

**Why the kill score can't be raised:** I confirmed the verification is *vacuous* — `__CPROVER_ensures(1==0)` still "verifies successfully," and even a guaranteed-OOB-read mutant survives, proving the body is never explored. The cause is the **replaced `UpdateHashValue` contract**, whose `requires` re-asserts `is_fresh(h, sizeof(ZopfliHash))` on an already-fresh `h`; in replacement/assertion mode that path becomes infeasible (assume-false-like), so everything after the first call — including the function exit where the postcondition is checked — is unreachable under the fixed `--depth 200`. I verified this is *not* the symbolic array-size: pinning `end == 4` didn't help. (Mutant 8, `pos - 0` vs `pos + 0`, is also semantically equivalent and unkillable regardless.)

This is the same documented `is_fresh`/depth vacuity family as the other `ZopfliHash` functions; I left the strongest functional spec in place and recorded the finding to memory.

Note: this function requires `-I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/stubs` (for the `FILE.h` stub) to compile under CBMC.
